questions.text: The word 'planet' is descended from the Greek word for:

answerChoices.text: wanderer
sphere
large mass
orbit

questions.text: To the Ancient Greeks, what differentiated planets from stars?

answerChoices.text: The planets do not always appear within the same constellation.
Only planets move throughout the night, stars do not.
Some stars are visible only some nights; every planets is visible every night.
Some planets are only visible some nights; every star is visible every night.

questions.text: The explanation of retrograde motion the Ptolemaic Universe is...

answerChoices.text: planets are moving in circles inside of circles.
the earth changed direction, leading to an apparent change in the direction of a planet.
the direction of gravity reversed for that planet.
the rotation of the planet reversed.

questions.text: What is the retrograde motion of a planet?

answerChoices.text: A planet appears to reverse direction in its course across the night sky.
The planet appears to speed up in its course across the night sky.
The planet appears to slow down in its course across the night sky.
The planet appears to stop in its course across the night sky.

questions.text: Assuming the Ancient Greeks could have discovered everything in the night sky, which of the following would _not_ be a planet under their conception?

answerChoices.text: Earth
Pluto
Neptune
Jupiter
